core_mechanism: Structural identifiability of a latent graph/tree topology from the observable joint distribution of leaf states generated by a Markov (state-transition) process on that graph: proving the map from hidden network structure to the induced pattern distribution is injective, so the underlying topology is uniquely recoverable from observed pattern frequencies (and two distinct structures yield distinguishable distributions).
brief_reason: The abstract asks whether the network topology is 'fully identifiable' from the distribution of leaf-patterns determined by a Markov substitution model and proves that a network and a tree cannot induce the same leaf-pattern distribution, which is exactly injectivity of the topology-to-distribution map.
